title: Swedish intelligence warns of increased Middle East war fallout
description:
STOCKHOLM: A top Swedish intelligence official on Thursday warned of increased threats against American and Israeli interests, as well as Iranian dissidents, in Sweden because of the war in the Middle East.
	The Scandinavian country’s security, which had already deteriorated after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, has worsened since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran on Saturday unleashed a new war, according to Fredrik Hallstrom, head of operations for

title: Kane to miss Bayern game against Gladbach with calf knock
description:
BERLIN: Harry Kane will miss Bayern Munich’s Bundesliga match against Borussia Moenchengladbach with a calf injury, coach Vincent Kompany said on Thursday.
	The England striker, who has been an ever-present for Bayern this season, should be fit in time for the Champions League last-16 first leg at Atalanta next Tuesday.
	“Harry Kane is out. He got a knock on his calf and hasn’t recovered yet,” Kompany said at a pre-match press conference ahead of

title: PSG prepare for Chelsea clash with Monaco rematch
description:
PARIS: Paris Saint-Germain warm up for their Champions League last-16 showdown with Chelsea by once again taking on Monaco on Friday, as coach Luis Enrique looked forward to “the best part of the season” starting.
	PSG have not managed to rediscover the exhilarating form that led them to Champions League glory for the first time in their history last season, but they come into March still on course to retain their French and European titles.

title: Macron calls on Israel, Iran and Hezbollah to prevent Lebanon being embroiled in conflict
description:
LONDON: Emmanuel Macron on Thursday called on Israel and Iran not to embroil Lebanon in the conflict sweeping the Middle East.

	The French president made his plea as panic swept through Beirut after Israel ordered residents to evacuate the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital and three villages in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region.

	Macron said he had drawn up a plan to end hostilities between Israel and the Iran-backed Lebanese militia Hezbol

title: Jordan’s king condemns Iranian attacks in region during talks with Syrian president
description:
LONDON: King Abdullah of Jordan and President Ahmad Al-Sharaa of Syria discussed on Thursday the dangerous developments in the region this week following the US and Israeli attacks against Iran that began on Saturday.

	During their telephone conversation, both leaders emphasized the need for dialogue to prevent further escalation of the regional crisis.

title: El-Sisi says Egypt in ‘state of near-emergency’ as war threatens economy
description:
CAIRO: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi said Thursday his country was in an economic “state of near-emergency” as a result of the Middle East war, warning of runaway inflation.
	The Arab world’s most populous nation has not been physically impacted by the US and Israeli war with Iran, which has seen strikes on Egypt’s wealthy Gulf allies and paralyzed trade through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: How Israel’s ‘buffer zone’ plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon
description:
BEIRUT: Lebanon faces what officials described as “one of the most dangerous moments in recent memory” after Hezbollah launched attacks on Israeli forces from areas both south and north of the Litani River, triggering a renewed Israeli air and ground campaign.

	Announcing its ground incursion into southern Lebanon, Israel said it aims to establish what it called a permanent “security zone” along the border.

title: Europol warns Iran crisis raises threat of terror, extremism and cyberattacks
description:
MADRID: The Middle East conflict will have “immediate repercussions” for European Union security with an increased threat of terrorism, serious and organized crime as well as violent extremism and cyberattacks, European police body Europol told Spanish news agency EFE on Thursday.
	Europol spokesman Jan Op Gen Oorth said he expected to see more cyberattacks against European infrastructure and an increase in online fraud using increasingly sophist

title: Trump fires homeland security chief Kristi Noem
description:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Thursday fired Kristi Noem as head of the Department of Homeland Security, the agency responsible for carrying out his sweeping immigration crackdown.
	According to multiple media reports, Trump was upset with Noem’s testimony at a Senate hearing this week where she said the president had approved a $220 million DHS advertising campaign in which she featured prominently.
	Trump, in a post on Truth Social,

title: Israel decided to kill Khamenei in November, defense minister says
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel took the decision to kill Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November and was planning to carry out the operation around six months later, Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday.
	Khamenei was killed in the first hours of the US-Israeli air campaign that began on Saturday in the first assassination of a country’s top ruler by an airstrike.

title: Lebanon said to seek one-month truce extension; Hezbollah again targets troops with drone
description:
Israel projects optimism ahead of planned talks on Thursday, as some exchanges of fire continue in south Lebanon; IDF says it killed 2 Hezbollah men who crossed ceasefire line
The post Lebanon said to seek one-month truce extension; Hezbollah again targets troops with drone appeared first on The Times of Israel.

Freed hostage performs, another is celebrated at Independence Day ceremony for outstanding soldiers; IDF chief: Israel ready 'to return immediately and forcefully' to fighting
The post Honoring soldiers, Herzog says they have fought battles future generations will study appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Blue skies, white smoke: Israelis celebrate 78 years of independence
description:
At beaches, city parks, nature areas and urban streets, the smell of grilled meat and the sound of party music fill the air as country marks another birthday
The post Blue skies, white smoke: Israelis celebrate 78 years of independence appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: A stroll through Tel Aviv on Independence Day: Amid fragile calm, a city exhales
description:
After weeks of war and amid tentative ceasefires, crowds fill beaches and parks, embracing barbecues, music and a sense of relief
The post A stroll through Tel Aviv on Independence Day: Amid fragile calm, a city exhales appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Residents pay the price as Afghanistan-Pakistan border remains closed
description: The border between Pakistan and Afghanistan has been completely closed for six months, a direct consequence of the conflict between Kabul and Islamabad. Despite several mediation attempts, negotiations have failed, and the two countries remain at a diplomatic impasse in a war that has already displaced tens of thousands of people and claimed numerous lives.

title: Food security: Why Guterres’s UN plan to get fertiliser flowing in Hormuz is stalling
description: The United Nations is pushing its proposal for a humanitarian corridor to get fertiliser and other essential goods through the Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively blocked since the start of the Iran war. This plan, which FRANCE 24 has seen, has yet to be approved by several countries.

title: Unblocked EU loan provides relief to Ukraine's strained finances
description: European Union ambassadors unblocked ⁠on Wednesday a ​90-billion-euro ($106 billion) loan to Ukraine that is critical for Kyiv's ability to keep up the fight against Russia. The loan, which ​was approved by the European Council in December, had been halted by a Hungarian veto amid a dispute over oil flows via the Druzhba pipeline.

title: 'Trump's high-stakes political theatre: US winning conventional war, Iran winning asymmetrical war'
description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Dr. William Lawrence, Senior Academic and Research Fellow-in-Residence and the Director of the North African Area Studies Program at the National Council on US-Arab Relations. As a former US diplomat in the Muslim world, he views what we are witnessing as a deliberate embrace of chaos and unpredictability as a coercive tool. Yet such theatrical instability carries diminishing returns when used as a modus opera

title: Pakistan out of women’s football tournament in India over political strife
description: Pakistani government bars football team from participating in the SAFF Womens Championship hosted in Goa, India.

title: The war on Iran: Breaking down the words you’re hearing
description: The terminology of the US-Israel war on Iran is drawn from a range of linguistic origins.

description: Moscow confirms it will suspend shipments of Kazakh oil to Germany from May 1.

description: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

title: Fires ignited by Ukrainian drones rage at Russian oil refinery
description: Hundreds of firefighters are trying to contain fires at Russia’s Tuapse oil refinery.

title: Nepal’s home minister resigns, second cabinet exit in one month
description: Sudan Gurung steps down after less than a month in office, citing questions about his investments ​and other matters.

title: On Independence Day, Israel Honors Rabbi Who Called to ‘Flatten’ Gaza
description: Avraham Zarbiv became known on Israel’s right for demolishing buildings in Gaza. He lit a torch at a national ceremony on Tuesday evening.

title: Возобновление переговоров США и Ирана возможно в ближайшие дни
description: Власти Пакистана полагают, что США и Иран могут провести новый раунд переговоров в течение последующих 36-72 часов, американский президент Дональд Трамп тоже считает возможным такое развитие событий, сообщает в среду New York Post."Источники в Исламабаде отмечают, что его посреднические усилия в работе с Тегераном приносят плоды, и вновь появилась возможность организовать мирные переговоры - в течение 36-72 часов", - информирует издание.

title: Трамп допустил новые переговоры с Ираном в ближайшие «36-72 часа»
description: Президент США Дональд Трамп анонсировал «хорошие новости» о втором раунде переговоров между американской и иранской сторонами 24 апреля. Об этом он сказал в интервью The New York Post.

title: From grief to trepidation: This year’s fateful transition from Memorial Day to Independence Day
description:

description: Swedish, Romanian and French fighters intercepted Russian Tu-22M3 ‘Backfire’ strategic bombers, carrying Kh-22N/Kh-32 supersonic cruise missiles, on two consecutive days over the Baltic Sea. For the third time in just over two years, Tupolev Tu-22M3 ‘Backfire’ strategic bombers of the Russian Aerospace Forces (RuAF) carrying Kh-32/Kh-22N supersonic cruise missiles were intercepted over the Baltic Sea []

description: The Bundeswehr will move away from rigid hardware quotas − the number of tanks, aircraft or ships − toward a flexible, effects-based planning model.

description: All discussion formats must be expanded to include other European states as soon as possible, argues guest author Karl-Heinz Kamp.

title: Cheri Cherin: Figure of the Congolese popular art movement honoured in Paris
description: In Paris, a powerful tribute celebrates one of the most influential figures in Congolese painting. Chéri Chérin, who passed away just a few months ago, leaves behind a vibrant and socially engaged body of work. A voice of the people and a keen observer of his time, he played a key role in bringing Congolese popular art to an international audience. FRANCE 24’s Awa Diouf and Anaïs Chesnel report.

title: Zimbabwe: 8000 schools receive internet under Presidential Scheme
description: In Zimbabwe, around 8000 schools are in the process of receiving internet access under the Presidential Internet Scheme. Thousands of antennas are to be installed in rural areas. The project, which started at the beginning of the year, could transform the lives of children, many of whom have never seen or used a computer linked to the internet.

title: Iranian economy: Layoffs, inflation add to sanctions pain amid war
description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Israel has created a profound economic crisis in Iran. As the government deals with the crippling internet blackouts and soaring inflation, a sense of uncertainty has taken hold of the nation's workforce.

title: In south Lebanon village, Hezbollah buries fighters killed in war with Israel
description: Hezbollah held a mass funeral in south Lebanon's Kfar Sir on Tuesday for more than a dozen fighters killed during more than six weeks of war with Israel. The ceremony came after Israel and Lebanon entered into a 10-day ceasefire on Friday announced by US President Donald Trump. Iran-backed Hezbollah has not provided the total number of its fighters killed since Israel last month launched a massive wave of strikes and a ground invasion of Lebanon.

title: Ukraine to restart oil flows via Druzhba pipeline, potentially unblocking EU's loan
description: Ukraine ​will resume pumping oil through the Druzhba pipeline on Wednesday, an industry source said, after President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said repairs were complete and urged the EU to unblock a 90-billion-euro loan halted by an argument over the pipe. A lengthy suspension of oil flows through the pipeline following a Russian attack on a pumping facility in Ukraine in January ​sparked an angry backlash ‌from European Union members Hungary and Slov

title: Devastated Gaza will need over $70 billion to rebuild, international report warns
description: Gaza will need over seventy billion dollars in the next decade to rebuild from more than two years of devastating conflict. That's from a report this week from the UN, the EU and the World Bank, which has looked at the damage done after Israel went to war with Hamas in the Palestinian enclave following its attack on October 7th 2023.

title: Peru's electoral commission chief resigns over chaotic vote
description: The head of Peru's electoral body resigned from his post Tuesday, hours before he was scheduled to be questioned by prosecutors over the chaotic presidential vote earlier this month. Peruvians went to the polls on April 12 to choose their ninth president in a decade, with the election dominated by voter anger at a political establishment seen as corrupt. The election day was marred by major logistical problems in the capital Lima, which prevented

title: EU gives preliminary approval to unblock 90-bn-euro Ukraine loan
description: EU member states on Wednesday gave a preliminary approval to unlock a €90 billion loan for Ukraine. The decision comes after delays linked to an oil dispute with Hungary over the Druzhba pipeline.

title: Nigeria faces jet fuel shortage as Strait of Hormuz disruption bites
description: Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies of middle distillates, a fuel marketers’ group said, squeezing airlines already grappling with soaring costs. Products such as jet fuel and diesel that normally pass through the strategic waterway have become less available worldwide, pushing up prices, the Major Energies Marketers Association of Nigeria (MEMAN) said.

title: UK Starmer says unaware of security lapse in Mandelson security vetting
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was grilled during questions in Parliament over his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain's top diplomat to Washington, effectively brushing aside security concerns. title: Pope calls for 'law and justice' on Equatorial Guinea visit
description: Pope Leo XIV on Tuesday urged Equatorial Guinea to place itself "in the service of law and justice", on the first day of his visit to the authoritarian country, which is one of the most closed-off states in Africa and regularly accused of human rights abuses. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.

title: Trump extends ceasefire with Iran, says Tehran regime 'seriously fractured'

title: Число мигрантов в ЕС достигло рекордных 64,2 млн
description: В 2025 году в странах Евросоюза проживало 64,2 млн иммигрантов, следует из доклада Центра исследований и анализа миграции (CReAM), подготовленного в партнерстве с благотворительной организацией Rockwool Foundation Berlin. description: Делегация высокопоставленных представителей госдепартамента США в этом месяце тайно посетила Кубу и обсудила с властями острова гуманитарный кризис и топливную блокаду. Об этом сообщила газета The New York Times со ссылкой на анонимные источники. Алехандро Гарсия дель Торо, заместитель директора министерства иностранных дел Кубы, отвечающий за отношения с США, также заявил государственной газете Granma, что встречи с американскими представителями

title: EMEA ports have rating headroom to weather Hormuz closure: Fitch
description:
RIYADH: Ports across Europe, the Middle East and Africa are broadly well-positioned to absorb the economic shock of a prolonged Iran conflict and a potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz, with most maintaining sufficient rating headroom, Fitch Ratings said.

	Despite the war significantly disrupting global oil and gas flows through the narrow channel along the Iranian coast, many ports in the region have robust financial profiles and diversifi

title: Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow and Kyiv escalate aerial barrages
description:
Russian air defenses downed 389 incoming Ukrainian drones, Russia’s Defense Ministry said Wednesday, in what was the largest reported overnight attack on Russian regions and Crimea since Moscow’s forces invaded Ukraine more than four years ago.
	The drones were stopped over 13 Russian regions as well as the Crimean Peninsula, which Russia illegally annexed from Ukraine in 2014.
	The attack underlined the growing capability of Ukraine’s domestical

title: Full card announced for PFL Chicago
description:
NEW YORK: The Professional Fighters League has announced the full fight card for PFL Chicago at Wintrust Arena on Saturday, April 11.

	In the main event, former Bellator bantamweight champion Sergio Pettis (25-7) meets undefeated rising contender Mitch McKee (10-0) in a pivotal 135-pound showdown. The co-main event features a middleweight clash as Milwaukee’s undefeated Jordan Newman (8-0) takes on American Top Team’s Josh Silveira (15-5).

title: Egypt’s Waterway plans to launch a real estate fund exceeding $190m
description:
LONDON: Egypt’s The Waterway Developments plans to establish a real estate investment fund worth 10 billion Egyptian pounds ($190.2 million) in the first half of this year to help finance its project spending, CEO Hossam Hassan told Asharq.

	The planned fund will be backed by income-generating real estate assets owned by the company across commercial, administrative, and residential segments, Hassan said, adding that the firm holds leased assets

title: PIF’s Folk Maritime redirects its routes to Red Sea to keep pace with shipping shifts
description: RIYADH: Amid the rapid shifts in regional maritime transport and the increasing rerouting of shipping away from the Arabian Gulf, Red Sea ports, particularly Jeddah Port, are emerging as key hubs for accommodating container traffic and enhancing the stability of supply chains in the region.

title: Deadliest year ever for Red Sea migrants with 922 deaths: UN
description:
ADDIS ABABA: The number of migrants who died on the “Eastern Route” from the Horn of Africa to the Arabian Peninsula doubled to a record high of 922 last year, the UN migration agency said Wednesday.
	Tens of thousands of migrants from Ethiopia, Somalia and neighboring countries take the route across the Red Sea each year, mostly from Djibouti to Yemen, in search of work as laborers or domestic workers in wealthy Gulf countries.

title: Fashion insiders Amina Muaddi, Loli Bahia celebrate Courreges designer
description:
DUBAI: After five years as Courreges’ artistic director, Nicolas Di Felice is stepping away from the brand as fashion insiders across the world take to social media to celebrate the Belgian designer.

	French Algerian model Loli Bahia and Jordanian Romanian shoe designer Amina Muaddi were among those paying tribute.

	Bahia took to Instagram to share a series of photographs from past campaigns with Courreges, one of which was included by Di Felic

title: Volunteer cook who pampers IDF troops under Hezbollah fire lights Independence Day torch
description:
Ora Hatan, whose mother was honored in 2008, is chosen for her 'determination' as she stays put on the Lebanese border, feeding soldiers even after a rocket destroyed her family restaurant
The post Volunteer cook who pampers IDF troops under Hezbollah fire lights Independence Day torch appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Vessels report being hit by gunfire as Iran says Strait of Hormuz shut again
description: Merchant vessels attempting to cross the strait on Saturday received radio messages from Iran’s navy telling them they were not allowed to pass.

title: China turns Taiwan’s own voices against it in information war
description: As Chinese warships and fighter jets staged massive drills around Taiwan in December, a parallel action was unfolding on smartphone screens.

title: Why supporters of Burkina Faso’s junta leader are campaigning against a Sky News journalist
description: Yousra Elbagir, a journalist with British TV channel Sky News, has become the target of a smear campaign by supporters of Burkina Faso's junta leader Captain Ibrahim Traoré after she raised questions about security in the country and the repression of free speech.

title: Ukraine says Druzhba pipeline running Russian oil to Europe can resume work
description: Kyiv hopeful pipeline restoration will unblock EU loan, with blocs foreign policy chief expecting positive decision.

title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
description: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.

title: Two Palestinians Killed in Israeli Shooting Near West Bank School
description: A child was among the dead, according to the Palestinian health ministry. The Israeli military said it had attempted to break up clashes in Al Mughayir, which has seen a surge of Israeli settler violence.

title: Israeli Soldiers Sentenced to Jail for Damaging Jesus Statue in Lebanon
description: A statue of the crucifixion of Christ has been replaced after one soldier photographed another swinging what looked like a sledgehammer at its head.

title: NYT: США планируют отправить делегацию в Россию для переговоров по Украине
description: Делегация США планирует совершить еще одну поездку в Россию для переговоров по урегулированию украинского кризиса. Об этом сообщает The New York Times со ссылкой на источник, знакомый с ходом переговоров.
